title: strychnos cerradoensis
intro: Strychnos cerradoensis, also known as the cerrado strychnine tree, is a species of plant in the family Loganiaceae. It is a medium-sized tree native to Brazil, characterized by its distinctive bark and small white flowers. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's seeds and bark are used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and improving digestion.
